content:
* We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation. For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day. We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region. We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands. We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture. We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe. Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui. Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région. Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales. Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine. Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________
sentences: * We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation.
For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day.
We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region.
We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands.
We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture.
We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe.
Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui.
Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région.
Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales.
Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine.

Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________

content:
* Need to do an inventory count at some point
* Internal: sponsorship package wording
* Wording for not-for-profit is misleading, can say something similar. Replace student federation with student association.
* Not sure what the priority event is about, especially for uOEC/CIuO.
* Social: Having returning sponsors would be incredible, having a relationship with companies is great to have.
* Comms: We just signed the contract with CU ads who reaches out to a ton of companies, if they sponsor through that they’re unlikely to sponsor 101 Week.
* Internal: Priority access to future events is probably better wording. What if we had a sort of legacy tier, CFES does it, maybe around 100k, they put in one sponsor and they get sponsors for the whole year.
* Internal: are we sending the same co-prez to PM and SDES?
* I think it should be both for both or one that goes to both. Connection reasons
* Ashna: Aiden’s just unavailable that weekend.
* Eric?: Maybe one goes to PM, and both goes to SDES if budget allows.
* Hayley: I think our External usually carries in terms of external relations anyway. I know there’s the Ontario group, but it should be okay.
* Internal: 101 week guide application form translations
* Check that last year's stuff is good instead of just assuming it is. Once something is flagged, why isn’t the form fixed yet?
* Hayley: I didn’t read the french form before sending it out. The guide form also didn’t work at submission.
* Eric: Everything we are putting out should be editable by Prez account at least
* Daniel: Maybe give Hayley access too? This is not Hayley’s problem but it’s related to her mandate in terms of maintaining public relations
* Hayley: Good to always have me editable for communication things like forms or posts. I am bilingual and can check French.
* Daniel: My reason for flagging this was because both presidents aren’t french. Maybe it’s better to have the English and French both in the same question instead of choosing at the start.
* Eric: I think the solution would be having Prez having edit access, and bilingual forms.
* Daniel: Currently why isn’t it fixed yet.
* Jake shares the form. Eric is going to put the translations in.
* Internal: can’t share docs with the competition@uottawaess.ca account
* Fixed: the account was uoec@uottawaess.ca and just got changed to competition@uottawaess.ca

content:
* Will put a poll in Discord with options
* Also let us know if anyone has ideas
* Idea 1: Mont-Tremblant area, only $400 a night, very pretty
* Can people drive and who has a car?
* Idea 2: Wesley Clover park camping within Ottawa, $400 for the whole weekend
* Electricity, no WiFi, but Maya lives 5 mins away
* We could bus there
* We could do 1 day of fun and 1 day of work
* Idea 3: YMCA
* Team bonding activities in the woods
* Hayley has been many times
* Idea 4: Calabogie
* Archery, obstacle courses, camping vibes, team bonding challenges
* Has WiFi
* Made for this kind of thing
* Idea 5: Bus to Toronto for fun
* We could go to Canada’s Wonderland
* Comms - What’s going in the hiring post?
* Graphic Design Committee? Luke isn’t here
* Secretaries? Yes
* Finance Commissioner? Already found
* ERTW Commissioner? Yes

content:
* Selection Committee? Yes, Commissioner hired amongst members
* Charity Committee? Yes, Commissioner hired amongst members but advertised separately
* Marketing Committee? No
* Francophone Outreach Commissioner? Not for now
* Please put discussions about posts in a thread in the future
* Comms - Blog? Didn’t know we had that. Not sure if we have the bandwidth to keep it up. Just have it as an online version of the otis?
* Adi will give Hayley access
* Consider renaming it from “Blog”
* Website will be reorganised soon
* Moving stuff from LinkTree to the website
* Adi is busy but will be adding stuff to the website soon
* Phil: Interest check for fundraising events throughout the summer (trivia nights, bar nights, game nights, etc)
* Once you hire a Manager
* Phil: offers have come in for collabs for post colour run carnival booth. Do we want to join? - if so, thinking of reaching out to CSSA/AÉI or SASA/AÉSA
* Jake: That cuts really close to Messy day. I can ask the 101 Week committee, but the chances are pretty low. We already have fundraising in the morning.
* Eric: We often have too much going on so we don’t go to a lot of the UOSU events, but we add them to our calendar. It’s up to you if you have the capacity to help with it. The concern would be if there’s overlapping schedules.
* Social: when to do summer DUSTED?
* Summer exams are the first two weeks of August
* Last year’s weekend worked fine: Aug 17th
* The 9th would be a good date since it’s after Session A-C exams and a few weeks before Session D exams
* Internal: ESS Discord?
* Everyone else has moved off of the SITE Discord. Do we want one too?
* Discord Committee? Under Comms?
* Eric made one but there’s no one in it
* Internal: BoD notice (positions available: Year reps, VP Franco)
* We need VP Franco applicants to fill out the test/interview form that Carolina made before the meeting with enough time for Hayden to conduct interviews
* Internal: Youth Leadership for Change grants
* Grant application for uOttawa - SOCIAL PLANNING COUNCIL OF OTTAWA
* Grant Guideline_ Youth Leadership for Change - Final..docx
* Daniel will send an announcement in Discord
